zone_6b_notes: Hardy perennial in Zone 6b — survives Farmington winters reliably with good drainage. Mulch with straw after hard freeze. Plants become woody after 3–4 years; divide or replace with rooted cuttings to maintain vigor. Harvest fresh sprigs throughout spring and summer; flavor is most intense just before flowering. Dried sage stores well. Berggarten (large-leafed, non-flowering) is popular for culinary use. Plant in well-drained soil — sage tolerates drought and poor soil far better than wet clay.

planting.best_planting_time: Transplant late May or purchase starts in spring

water: drought tolerant — 0.5–1 inch/week; excellent drainage required
